TE Operational Flow Order -- LIFTED EFF 4/6


Notice Text:

Effective 9:00 AM CCT April 6, 2026, Texas Eastern (TE) is lifting the previously posted Operational Flow Order (OFO) for Access Area, M1-24, M1-30, M2-24, M2-30 and M3.

 

Shippers across all Texas Eastern's Access Area Zones and well as Market Area Zones are required to coordinate any imbalance payback through TE's account representative and actual payback quantities may be limited depending on system operations and conditions. If customers do not adhere to the imbalance requirements stated above, TE will utilize any provision of its tariff to ensure system integrity including the issuance of customer specific or system wide OFOs.

 

Please contact your Operations Account Representative should you have any questions.

 

Please refer to the OFO table below for the effected TETCO zones, dates, and tolerance percentages.

 

Zone

Gas Day April 5

Gas Day April 6 and until further notice

M1-24"

OFO in Effect:

No Due Shipper

8%/250

No OFO*

M1-30"

OFO in Effect:

No Due Shipper

8%/250

No OFO*

M2-24"

OFO in Effect:

No Due Shipper

5%/250

No OFO*

M2-30"

OFO in Effect:

No Due Shipper

5%/250

No OFO*

M3

OFO in Effect:

No Due Shipper

5%/250

No OFO*

ETX

OFO in Effect:

No Due Shipper

8%/250

No OFO*

STX

OFO in Effect:

No Due Shipper

8%/250

No OFO*

WLA

OFO in Effect:

No Due Shipper

8%/250

No OFO*

ELA

OFO in Effect:

No Due Shipper

8%/250

No OFO*

Customer Specific

System Wide OFO in Effect

No OFO

 

*As noted above, all imbalance payback must be coordinated through TE's account representative.  In the event uncoordinated imbalance payback occurs and it jeopardizes system operations, customer specific OFOs may be required.

 

NOTE:

Deliveries No DP : Shippers are not to take more than scheduled

Deliveries No DS : Shippers are not to take less than scheduled

Receipts No DP : Shippers are not to give less than scheduled

Receipts No DS : Shippers are not to give more than scheduled
